annotations.label: Matrix multiplication

anchor.snippet: matrix multiplication is a binary operation that produces a matrix from two matrices

annotations.note: The heterogeneous matrix multiplication instance `HMul (Matrix l m α) (Matrix m n α) (Matrix l n α)` is defined directly in `Mathlib/Data/Matrix/Mul.lean`.

annotations.label: Matrix product (m×n times n×p)

anchor.snippet: the matrix product C = AB (denoted without multiplication signs or dots) is defined to be the m × p matrix

annotations.note: The `HMul` instance and `Matrix.mul_apply` give exactly the m×p product matrix of an m×n and n×p matrix.

anchors.snippet: the matrix product C = AB (denoted without multiplication signs or dots) is defined to be the m × p matrix

annotations.label: Entry of product is dot product of row and column

anchor.snippet: is the dot product of the i th row of A and the j th column of B

annotations.note: `Matrix.mul_apply` states `(M * N) i k = ∑ j, M i j * N j k`, exactly the row-times-column dot product.

annotations.label: Existence condition for matrix product

anchor.snippet: the product AB is defined if and only if the number of columns in A equals the number of rows in B

annotations.note: The dimension-matching condition is enforced at the type level via the `HMul (Matrix l m α) (Matrix m n α)` instance rather than stated as a proposition.

annotations.label: Matrix-times-vector product

anchor.snippet: the matrix-times-vector product denoted by

annotations.note: `Matrix.mulVec` (notation `M *ᵥ v`) is defined as the row-wise dot product of `M` against `v`.

annotations.label: Vector-times-matrix product

anchor.snippet: can be viewed as a row vector

annotations.note: `Matrix.vecMul` (notation `v ᵥ* M`) is defined as the column-wise dot product of `v` against `M`.

annotations.label: Dot product as matrix multiplication

anchor.snippet: the dot product (or inner product)

annotations.note: `Matrix.dotProduct v w = ∑ i, v i * w i` is defined and `Matrix.mul_apply` ties it back to the 1×1 matrix entry of the product.

annotations.label: Outer product of vectors

anchor.snippet: also known as outer-product

annotations.note: `Matrix.vecMulVec w v i j = w i * v j` is exactly the outer product.

annotations.label: Illustration of matrix product

anchor.snippet: illustrates diagrammatically the product of two matrices

annotations.note: A diagrammatic illustration with no formal mathematical content to formalize.

anchors.snippet: illustrates diagrammatically the product of two matrices

annotations.label: Matrix product represents composition of linear maps

anchor.snippet: the matrix of the composite map

annotations.note: `Matrix.toLin_mul` and `Matrix.toLin'_mul` show that `toLin (A * B) = toLin A ∘ₗ toLin B`.

annotations.label: Composition of rotations

anchor.snippet: the composition corresponds to the rotation by angle

annotations.note: Mathlib has conformal/rotation matrices and angle-addition formulas, but the specific worked example of multiplying two 2D rotation matrices is not given as a single declaration.

anchors.snippet: the composition corresponds to the rotation by angle

annotations.label: Resource allocation factory example

anchor.snippet: a fictitious factory uses 4 kinds of basic commodities

annotations.note: An applied numerical example without a corresponding Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: System of linear equations as matrix equation

anchor.snippet: such a system is equivalent with the single matrix equation

annotations.note: `Matrix.mulVec` encodes `Ax`, so a linear system becomes `A *ᵥ x = b`, but there is no explicit equivalence theorem between sum-of-equations form and matrix-equation form.

annotations.label: Dot product as matrix product

anchor.section: Dot product, bilinear form and sesquilinear form
anchor.snippet: The dot product of two column vectors is the unique entry of the matrix product

annotations.note: `Matrix.dotProduct` plus `Matrix.mul_apply` yield this identification, but no single lemma states the dot product equals the unique entry of `xᵀ y`.

annotations.label: Bilinear form as matrix product

anchor.section: Dot product, bilinear form and sesquilinear form
anchor.snippet: any bilinear form over a vector space of finite dimension may be expressed as a matrix product

annotations.note: `Matrix.toBilin` gives a linear equivalence between matrices and bilinear forms on a finite-dim space, with `Matrix.toBilin_apply` expressing the form as `xᵀ M y`.

annotations.label: Sesquilinear form as matrix product

anchor.section: Dot product, bilinear form and sesquilinear form
anchor.snippet: any sesquilinear form may be expressed as

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrix₂` and `Matrix.toLinearMap₂` give the matrix representation of sesquilinear forms in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/SesquilinearForm.lean`.

annotations.label: Non-commutativity of matrix multiplication

anchor.snippet: if A and B are square matrices of the same size, are both products defined and of the same size

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Matrix.instRing` deliberately does not provide commutativity for `n ≥ 2`, but there is no explicit `¬ Commute A B` example as a named lemma.

annotations.label: Center characterization via commuting with all matrices

anchor.snippet: This example may be expanded for showing that

annotations.note: `Matrix.center_eq_scalar_image` (and `Matrix.subalgebraCenter_eq_scalarAlgHom_map`) state that the center of the matrix ring is exactly the scalar matrices.

annotations.label: Diagonal matrices commute

anchor.snippet: when D and E are two (square) diagonal matrices (of the same size); then DE = ED

annotations.note: `Matrix.commute_diagonal` proves `Commute (diagonal d₁) (diagonal d₂)` over a commutative semiring, with `Matrix.diagonal_mul_diagonal` giving the explicit product.

annotations.label: Distributivity of matrix product over addition

anchor.snippet: The matrix product is distributive with respect to matrix addition

annotations.note: `Matrix.mul_add` and `Matrix.add_mul` provide left- and right-distributivity of matrix multiplication.

annotations.label: Product with a scalar

anchor.snippet: are obtained by left or right multiplying all entries of A by c

annotations.note: `Matrix.smul_mul` and `Matrix.mul_smul` express `(a • M) * N = a • (M * N) = M * (a • N)`.

annotations.label: Transpose of a product

anchor.snippet: the transpose of a product of matrices is the product, in the reverse order, of the transposes of the factors

annotations.note: `Matrix.transpose_mul` proves `(M * N)ᵀ = Nᵀ * Mᵀ`.

annotations.label: Conjugate of a product

anchor.snippet: If A and B have complex entries

annotations.note: `Matrix.map_mul` states `(L * M).map f = L.map f * M.map f` for any (non-unital) ring hom, so applying it to `starRingEnd ℂ` gives the entry-wise conjugate-of-a-product identity.

annotations.label: Conjugate transpose of a product

anchor.snippet: Transposition acts on the indices of the entries, while conjugation acts independently on the entries themselves

annotations.note: `Matrix.conjTranspose_mul` proves `(M * N)ᴴ = Nᴴ * Mᴴ`.

annotations.label: Associativity of matrix multiplication

anchor.snippet: one has the associative property

annotations.note: `Matrix.mul_assoc` proves `L * M * N = L * (M * N)`.

annotations.label: Parenthesization affects computational cost

anchor.snippet: if A , B and C are matrices of respective sizes 10×30, 30×5, 5×60

annotations.note: An arithmetic remark about flop counts with no corresponding Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Similarity transformation

anchor.snippet: defines a similarity transformation

annotations.note: Mathlib uses unit conjugation `P⁻¹ * A * P` directly (e.g. in `Matrix.charpoly_units_conj`, `Matrix.trace_units_conj`) but does not introduce a named `Matrix.Similar` relation.

annotations.label: Similarity transformations preserve products

anchor.snippet: Similarity transformations map product to products

annotations.note: Follows from `Matrix.mul_assoc` together with `Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul`, but is not stated as a single dedicated lemma.

annotations.label: Square matrices form a ring

anchor.snippet: This makes

annotations.note: `Matrix.instRing` (and `Matrix.semiring`, `Matrix.nonAssocSemiring`) make `Matrix n n α` a ring whenever `α` is.

annotations.label: Invertible and singular matrices

anchor.snippet: A matrix that has an inverse is an invertible matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det` and `Matrix.Invertible` capture the invertible/singular distinction via `IsUnit`.

annotations.label: Invertibility of a product

anchor.snippet: A product of matrices is invertible if and only if each factor is invertible

annotations.note: Provable by combining `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det` with `Matrix.det_mul` and `IsUnit.mul_iff`, but no dedicated `Matrix.isUnit_mul_iff` lemma was located.

annotations.label: Determinant of a product

anchor.snippet: the determinant of a product is the product of the determinants

annotations.note: `Matrix.det_mul` proves `det (M * N) = det M * det N`.

annotations.label: AB and BA share invariants

anchor.snippet: AB and BA have the same trace , the same characteristic polynomial , and the same eigenvalues with the same multiplicities

annotations.note: `Matrix.trace_mul_comm` gives equal traces and `Matrix.charpoly_mul_comm` gives equal characteristic polynomials (and hence equal eigenvalue multiplicities).

annotations.label: Powers of a matrix

anchor.snippet: One may raise a square matrix to any nonnegative integer power multiplying it by itself repeatedly

annotations.note: Since `Matrix n n α` is a `Monoid`/`Semiring`, the generic `Monoid.npow` and `pow_succ` give natural-number powers of square matrices.

annotations.label: Power of a diagonal matrix

anchor.snippet: the k th power of a diagonal matrix is obtained by raising the entries to the power k

annotations.note: `Matrix.diagonal_pow` states `(diagonal v) ^ k = diagonal (v ^ k)`.

annotations.label: Matrices over a ring form a ring

anchor.snippet: the n × n matrices over a ring form a ring

annotations.note: `Matrix.instRing` provides the ring structure on `Matrix n n α` for `α` a ring.

annotations.label: Invertibility via determinant over commutative ring

anchor.snippet: a matrix has an inverse if and only if its determinant has a multiplicative inverse in R

annotations.note: `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det` proves `IsUnit A ↔ IsUnit A.det` over a commutative ring.

annotations.label: Matrix groups

anchor.snippet: form a group under matrix multiplication, the subgroups of which are called matrix groups

annotations.note: `Matrix.GeneralLinearGroup` (notation `GL n R`) is defined as the units of the matrix ring; the general notion of a matrix group as an arbitrary subgroup of `GL` is not given its own definition.

annotations.label: Category of matrices

anchor.snippet: Matrices are the morphisms of a category , the category of matrices

annotations.note: `CategoryTheory.Mat_ C` is the additive envelope where morphisms are matrices of morphisms in a preadditive category; the file's docstring notes that a more convenient `Mat R` over a ring is future work.

annotations.label: Naive matrix multiplication complexity

anchor.snippet: The matrix multiplication algorithm that results from the definition requires

annotations.note: A complexity claim about the naive O(n³) algorithm with no corresponding declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Strassen's algorithm

anchor.snippet: shown in 1969 by Volker Strassen , who provided an algorithm, now called Strassen's algorithm

annotations.note: Strassen's algorithm is only mentioned in a comment in `Mathlib/Computability/AkraBazzi/AkraBazzi.lean`; it is not formalized.
